summary: A Go-based orchestration and testing framework utilizing AI agents to automatically generate and validate test suites via MCP.
concerns: High volume of auto-generated 'extra tests' from glm-agents may clutter the codebase and increase maintenance overhead
Frequent metadata and session archival commits suggest heavy CI/agent orchestration overhead
Potential redundancy between baseline tests and agent-generated extra tests
suggestions: Consolidate or prune redundant agent-generated test files to maintain a clean repository
Evaluate the coverage delta provided by 'extra tests' versus the baseline to justify their inclusion
Monitor the performance impact of the MCP handlers under load
